content_standards.statement: Listen to and critically evaluate complex spoken texts, identifying bias, persuasion techniques and logical fallacies.

indicators.statement: Listen to a persuasive speech or advertisement and identify the persuasive techniques used (emotional appeal, bandwagon, testimonial, repetition).
exemplars: Listen to radio or TV advertisements and identify persuasive strategies.
Evaluate whether a speaker's argument is logically sound or contains bias.

indicators.statement: Listen to an interview or panel discussion and take structured notes capturing key arguments, counterarguments and conclusions.
exemplars: Listen to a recorded interview and organise notes under main points and details.
Summarise the key takeaways from a panel discussion in bullet form.

content_standards.statement: Deliver persuasive and informative speeches with confidence, using rhetorical devices and audience engagement strategies.

indicators.statement: Prepare and deliver a persuasive speech of 5-7 minutes on a social issue, using rhetorical questions, repetition and call to action.
exemplars: Write and deliver a speech on 'Why Every Child Deserves Quality Education'.
Use rhetorical devices and vary tone and pace for emphasis.

indicators.statement: Participate in a mock interview, answering questions clearly and confidently while demonstrating proper etiquette and body language.
exemplars: Role-play a job or school admission interview with appropriate responses.
Evaluate peers' interview performance using a checklist.

content_standards.statement: Lead and participate in parliamentary-style debates and academic discussions with sophisticated argumentation.

indicators.statement: Lead a group discussion effectively by setting the agenda, managing contributions, summarising points and drawing conclusions.
exemplars: Chair a group discussion on a school policy issue and produce a group report.
Practise skills of a good discussion leader: impartiality, time management, summarising.

indicators.statement: Engage in a parliamentary-style debate with points of order, rebuttal and summation, evaluating the strength of arguments presented.
exemplars: Conduct a full debate with Speaker, proposers and opponents.
Score debates using criteria: content, delivery, rebuttal effectiveness.

content_standards.statement: Read and critically analyse complex texts, evaluating arguments, synthesising information from multiple sources and making judgments.

indicators.statement: Read expository and argumentative texts, identify the thesis, evaluate the strength of evidence and detect logical fallacies or weaknesses.
exemplars: Read an editorial and evaluate whether conclusions are well-supported by evidence.
Identify examples of weak arguments, generalisations or bias in written texts.

indicators.statement: Synthesise information from two or more sources on the same topic, comparing perspectives and drawing informed conclusions.
exemplars: Read two articles on the same issue and write a synthesis paragraph.
Create a Venn diagram to compare perspectives from different sources.

indicators.statement: Read and interpret a variety of non-continuous texts (charts, graphs, tables, infographics, maps) and integrate information with continuous texts.
exemplars: Interpret data from a graph and write a paragraph explaining the trends.
Answer comprehension questions that require reading both text and visual data.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate mastery of a wide range of vocabulary including academic language, subject-specific terminology and nuanced word usage.

indicators.statement: Use academic vocabulary and subject-specific terminology accurately in discussions, presentations and written work across the curriculum.
exemplars: Build a personal academic word bank from reading across subjects.
Use academic vocabulary (analyse, evaluate, synthesise, contrast) in essays.

indicators.statement: Distinguish between denotation (dictionary meaning) and connotation (associated meaning) of words and use them deliberately for effect.
exemplars: Compare words with similar denotations but different connotations (e.g., slim/skinny, assertive/bossy).
Explain how connotation affects the tone and mood of writing.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate mastery of advanced grammatical structures including conditionals, gerunds, infinitives and phrasal verbs.

indicators.statement: Use second and third conditional sentences to express hypothetical and unreal past situations.
exemplars: Write second conditional sentences (If I were president, I would...).
Write third conditional sentences about unreal past situations and discuss regrets.

indicators.statement: Distinguish between gerunds (-ing form as noun) and infinitives (to + verb) and use them correctly after specific verbs and prepositions.
exemplars: Classify verbs that take gerunds (enjoy, avoid, consider) vs infinitives (want, hope, decide).
Complete sentences choosing between gerund and infinitive forms.

indicators.statement: Use a wide range of phrasal verbs and collocations accurately in spoken and written English.
exemplars: Match phrasal verbs with their meanings and use them in sentences.
Identify common collocations (make a decision, do homework) and avoid errors.

content_standards.statement: Transform sentences using a variety of techniques including cleft sentences, emphatic structures and sentence reduction.

indicators.statement: Transform sentences between direct and indirect speech, applying all rules for tense changes, pronoun shifts and time expressions.
exemplars: Convert dialogues from direct to indirect speech accurately.
Transform reported questions and commands back to direct speech.

indicators.statement: Combine and rearrange sentences using participial phrases, appositives and absolute constructions for conciseness and variety.
exemplars: Use present and past participial phrases to combine sentences.
Identify and use appositive phrases to add information without extra clauses.

content_standards.statement: Identify and correct common grammatical errors including Ghanaianisms and frequently confused words.

indicators.statement: Identify and correct common grammatical errors and Ghanaianisms in speech and writing (e.g., 'I am coming' for 'I am going', tense misuse, wrong prepositions).
exemplars: Correct common Ghanaian English errors in given passages.
Distinguish between commonly confused words (affect/effect, loose/lose, its/it's).

content_standards.statement: Write well-structured essays of various types (narrative, descriptive, argumentative, expository) at BECE standard.

indicators.statement: Write a minimum 450-word argumentative essay with a clear thesis statement, at least three body paragraphs with topic sentences, evidence and analysis, and a strong conclusion.
exemplars: Write a full-length argumentative essay on a BECE-type topic.
Self-assess and peer-assess essays using a BECE-aligned marking rubric.

indicators.statement: Write a narrative essay with a complex plot, well-developed characters, effective use of dialogue and figurative language, and a moral or lesson.
exemplars: Write a narrative on 'A Day I Will Never Forget' using flashback or foreshadowing.
Include varied sentence structures, dialogue and at least three literary devices.

content_standards.statement: Write formal and semi-formal texts for specific real-world purposes and audiences.

indicators.statement: Write formal letters of application, complaint and request, using appropriate format, register and persuasive language.
exemplars: Write a letter of application for a school prefect position.
Write a letter of complaint about a product or service.

indicators.statement: Write a speech for a school function (speech day, prize-giving, farewell) with appropriate opening, body and closing.
exemplars: Write a farewell speech for the outgoing JHS 3 class.
Write a vote of thanks for a school occasion.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate the ability to summarise given passages concisely and answer comprehension questions accurately at BECE standard.

indicators.statement: Summarise a given passage in a specified number of sentences, capturing all main points without including unnecessary details.
exemplars: Summarise a 300-word passage in not more than 5 sentences.
Practise BECE-style summary questions under timed conditions.

indicators.statement: Answer structured comprehension questions requiring literal, inferential, evaluative and applied responses.
exemplars: Practise answering comprehension questions at different cognitive levels.
Identify question types and apply appropriate answering techniques.

content_standards.statement: Critically analyse literary texts, discussing themes, character development, style and the writer's craft.

indicators.statement: Analyse a novel or short story, discussing themes, character development (static vs dynamic), narrative perspective and the author's message.
exemplars: Write a character analysis of a major character from a studied text.
Discuss how the author uses setting and symbolism to develop themes.

indicators.statement: Analyse poems for theme, tone, mood, figurative language and sound devices, explaining how they contribute to meaning.
exemplars: Write a critical appreciation of a studied poem discussing all literary elements.
Compare two poems on a similar theme, analysing differences in style and language.

indicators.statement: Read and analyse a play, discussing dramatic techniques, conflict, character motivation and the playwright's message.
exemplars: Perform and discuss scenes from a studied play.
Write an essay on the central conflict and its resolution in a drama text.
